items.label: Getting Started
items.content:
# ZYNQ

## Installation

ZYNQ is a WebRTC + games framework.
It combines peer-to-peer communication with a game library system.

No backend required.

items.label: Script Tag
items.content:
## Script Tag

Loads ZYNQ globally into `window.ZYNQ`.

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.1.1/index.min.js"></script>
```

After loading:
```js
window.ZYNQ
```

items.label: ES Module
items.content:
## ES Module (Side Effect Import)

This version only initializes global `window.ZYNQ`.

```js
import "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.1.1/index.min.js"
```

After import:
```js
window.ZYNQ.Peer
window.ZYNQ.games
```

items.label: Game Object
items.content:
## ZYNQ.games

### Game Object Structure

Represents a single game entry.

```ts
Game {
  name: string,
  alias: string,
  url: string,
  thumb: string,
  category?: string,
  devices?: number[],

  isSupported: boolean
}
```

items.label: State & Getters
items.content:
# ZYNQ

## ZYNQ.games

### State

```ts
config: {
  src: string,
  cdn: string
}

all: Game[]
filtered: Game[]
favorites: Set<string>
deviceType: number // 1: PC Touch, 2: PC No Touch, 3: Phone, 4: Tablet
history: string[]
```

### Getters

```ts
list: Game[]
total: number
filteredCount: number
```

children.label: .init()
children.content:
## init(options?)
Loads game database from CDN.

```js
await ZYNQ.games.init()
```

children.label: .search()
children.content:
## search(query)
Filters games.

```js
ZYNQ.games.search("alien")
```

children.label: .random()
children.content:
## random(limit)
Random subset.

```js
ZYNQ.games.random(3)
```

children.label: .getRandomOne()
children.content:
## getRandomOne()
Single random game.

```js
ZYNQ.games.getRandomOne()
```

children.label: .shuffle()
children.content:
## shuffle()
Shuffle list.

```js
ZYNQ.games.shuffle()
```

children.label: .sortBy()
children.content:
## sortBy(field)
Sort games.

```js
ZYNQ.games.sortBy("name")
```

children.label: .sortByPopularity()
children.content:
## sortByPopularity()
Sort by popularity.

```js
ZYNQ.games.sortByPopularity()
```

children.label: .getByAlias()
children.content:
## getByAlias(alias)
Find game.

```js
ZYNQ.games.getByAlias("alien-hominid")
```

children.label: .getByCategory()
children.content:
## getByCategory(category)
Filter by category.

```js
ZYNQ.games.getByCategory("shooter")
```

children.label: .filterSupported()
children.content:
## filterSupported()
Only supported games.

```js
ZYNQ.games.filterSupported()
```

children.label: .filterFavorites()
children.content:
## filterFavorites()
Only favorites.

```js
ZYNQ.games.filterFavorites()
```

children.label: .toggleFavorite()
children.content:
## toggleFavorite(alias)
Toggle favorite state.

```js
ZYNQ.games.toggleFavorite("alien-hominid")
```

children.label: History API
children.content:
## History API

### addToHistory(alias)
```js
ZYNQ.games.addToHistory("alien-hominid")
```

### getHistory()
```js
ZYNQ.games.getHistory()
```

### clearHistory()
```js
ZYNQ.games.clearHistory()
```

children.label: Utilities
children.content:
## Utilities

### reset()
```js
ZYNQ.games.reset()
```

### isFavorite(alias)
```js
ZYNQ.games.isFavorite("alien-hominid")
```

groups.title: ZYNQ Peer
groups.description: Lightweight peer-to-peer wrapper over PeerJS with event-based API for connections, messaging, and media streams.

items.label: Constructor
items.content:
## ZYNQ.Peer
Creates a new peer instance.

```js
const peer = new ZYNQ.Peer({
  video: false,
  audio: false,
  txt: true
})
```

### Options
- video: enable video stream
- audio: enable audio stream
- txt: enable data connections
- id: optional custom peer id

children.label: connect(peerId)
children.content:
Establishes a data connection to another peer.

```js
peer.connect("abc-123")
```

children.label: send(peerId, data)
children.content:
Sends a message to a connected peer. If not connected yet, it queues and auto-connects.

```js
peer.send("abc-123", "hello")
```

children.label: call(peerId)
children.content:
Starts a media call (audio/video) with another peer.

```js
peer.call("abc-123", { video: true, audio: true })
```

children.label: disconnect(peerId)
children.content:
Closes connection and disables auto-reconnect for that peer.

```js
peer.disconnect("abc-123")
```

children.label: ready
open
message
stream
close
disconnect
children.content:
Triggered when a peer fully disconnects and auto-reconnect is disabled or failed.

```js
peer.on('disconnect', ({ id }) => {})
```

children.label: error
reconnecting
